cards.back: Produces ATP through cellular respiration — the powerhouse of the cell
cards.correct_answer_hint: The mitochondria produces ATP, it is the powerhouse of the cell

cards.front: What is the function of the hippocampus?
cards.correct_answer_hint: The hippocampus is responsible for memory consolidation and spatial navigation
The pancreas secretes insulin and glucagon for blood sugar regulation, and digestive enzymes

cards.front: Where is the brachial plexus located?
cards.back: Network of nerves from C5-T1, running through the neck and armpit
cards.correct_answer_hint: The brachial plexus is a network of nerves originating from C5 to T1 in the neck and armpit

cards.front: What is the role of the sinoatrial node?
cards.back: The heart's natural pacemaker — generates the electrical impulse that starts each heartbeat
cards.correct_answer_hint: The sinoatrial node is the natural pacemaker of the heart, generating the electrical impulse for each heartbeat

cards.front: What does the thyroid gland regulate?
cards.back: Metabolism, heart rate, and body temperature via T3/T4 hormones
cards.correct_answer_hint: The thyroid regulates metabolism, heart rate, and body temperature through T3 and T4 hormones
